result:
I would give every child, everywhere, the irrevocable guarantee of a safe place to sleep, enough to eat, and a year of high-quality, play-based preschool before age five.  

Those three things—safety, nutrition, and early stimulation—are the single highest-leverage intervention we know for raising collective IQ, lowering violence, and breaking inter-generational poverty. The brain is most plastic in the first thousand days; once that window closes you can spend decades trying to patch the gaps.  

It isn’t flashy, it doesn’t require new physics, and it would pay for itself within a generation through higher tax receipts and lower crime, health, and welfare costs. We already have the recipes; we just treat them as charity instead of infrastructure.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
